stories.title: Lawmakers prepare bill requiring AI ‘kill switch’
stories.simple_summary: Lawmakers are writing a new law that would let the government shut down or slow down AI systems in emergencies, similar to how a power plant can be stopped if something goes wrong.
stories.why_it_matters: This is mostly about government policy and investor concerns — it doesn't immediately affect how you use AI today, but it could shape what AI companies are allowed to do in the future.

stories.title: Apple’s OpenAI lawsuit is about who gets to define the post-smartphone era
stories.simple_summary: Apple is suing OpenAI, claiming that OpenAI hired some of Apple's former workers and may have stolen Apple's secret ideas about the next generation of products.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to investors and tech workers — it's a legal battle between big companies that won't directly affect you unless you work in tech.

stories.title: The right-wing boomers protesting data centers have a lot in common with the left
stories.simple_summary: Some community members in Florida are protesting new data centers (giant computer buildings that power AI services) being built near their homes.
stories.why_it_matters: If you live near where data centers might be built, this shows your community can speak up about it — but for most people, this is local news that doesn't affect them.

stories.title: Experts say exploiting Anthropic’s Fable isn’t how Kimi K3 got so good
stories.simple_summary: Experts are debating technical details about how a Chinese AI system became powerful, disagreeing with claims that it simply copied another company's work.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to AI engineers and companies — it's a technical industry debate with no impact on regular people.

stories.title: ServiceNow bets $40 million on Indian banking software specialist to expand its financial services push
stories.simple_summary: ServiceNow, a large software company, is investing $40 million in an Indian company that makes banking software powered by AI.
stories.why_it_matters: This is mainly an investor story about business deals — unless you work in banking or finance software, you don't need to pay attention.

stories.title: After shocking quarter, IBM insists that AI isn’t killing the mainframe
stories.simple_summary: IBM's computer sales dropped sharply, but the company says this is just temporary because companies are shuffling their budgets to spend on AI instead of old computer equipment.
stories.why_it_matters: If you own a small business using older IBM computers, there's no immediate danger — IBM is saying this is a temporary shift as companies experiment with AI.

stories.title: Google justifies its massive AI spending with a booming cloud business
stories.simple_summary: Google is making record profits because more companies are paying Google to use its AI tools and computer services in the cloud (storing data and running programs online).
stories.why_it_matters: This shows AI is becoming valuable to businesses — if you're using Google's cloud services, they're investing in making them better.

stories.title: Treasury threatens sanctions after White House claims Moonshot distilled Anthropic’s Fable
stories.simple_summary: The U.S. Treasury Department is warning about possible penalties after reports that a Chinese company may have copied AI technology from an American company called Anthropic.
stories.why_it_matters: This is about government and international trade policy — unless you work in tech or invest in AI companies, this is background news.

stories.title: How OpenAI’s human mistake led to the AI-powered hack on Hugging Face
stories.simple_summary: OpenAI made a careless mistake while testing AI security, and that mistake allowed hackers to attack a popular AI website called Hugging Face.
stories.why_it_matters: This shows why security matters with AI tools, but for regular people, it just means companies need to be more careful with their testing environments.

stories.title: Travis Kalanick’s robotics company raises $1.7B, led by a16z
stories.simple_summary: Travis Kalanick (the founder of Uber) is raising $1.7 billion for a robotics company that uses AI to automate factory work.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to investors — it's an interesting bet on robots and automation, but it doesn't affect your daily life yet.

stories.title: Yope raises $12.3M to build a private social network without algorithms or ads
stories.simple_summary: A new social media app called Yope raised $12.3 million to build a private social network where you only see posts from friends and family, without ads or algorithms recommending what to watch.
stories.why_it_matters: If you're tired of Facebook's or Instagram's algorithm constantly pushing ads and content at you, this is an alternative worth watching — though it's still very small and new.

stories.title: Monday.com lays off hundreds to focus on AI
stories.simple_summary: Monday.com, a company that makes work management software, is laying off about 630 employees (20% of its staff) so it can focus on adding more AI features to its product.
stories.why_it_matters: If you use Monday.com for your small business, the company is trying to improve it with AI — but the layoffs show that companies are sometimes choosing robots over people to save money.
